reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21: math-formula OCR correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (transformers.js image-to-text WASM q8; Donut-Swin encoder + mBART merged decoder), then the deployed page driven end-to-end. Rendered equations recover valid, correctly-structured LaTeX (Pythagoras, a fraction, a definite integral, the quadratic formula). Samples are first-party procedural PNGs (LaTeX typeset via MathJax then rasterised).

rubric.evidence: Correctness verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js image-to-text, WASM q8) BEFORE building. texify recovers valid, correctly-structured LaTeX from equation images: x^2+y^2=z^2 -> $$x^{2}+y^{2}=z^{2}$$; \frac{a+b}{c} exactly; \int_0^1 x^2 dx -> \int_{0}^{1}x^{2}\,dx=\frac{1}{3}; the quadratic formula -> x={\frac{-b\pm{\sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}}{2a}}. Deployed page (scripts/validate-math-formula-ocr.mjs 10/10): clicking sample equations generates the right LaTeX (Pythagoras, integral, quadratic). Every character is the model's real decoded output, streamed token by token, not canned.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from the built OCR families: nougat converts whole academic pages to markdown, trocr reads printed text lines, mgp-str reads scene text - this is a dedicated math-formula -> LaTeX recogniser.

rubric.evidence: Input an equation IMAGE -> output a LaTeX string generated autoregressively (vision-encoder-decoder). A distinct capability + I/O from the built image-to-text (vit-gpt2 captioning writes a sentence; donut-docvqa answers a question): this emits structured markup (LaTeX) that reproduces the maths.
rubric.notes: The page strips the $$…$$ display delimiters for a clean, copyable body and re-typesets the result for a visual round-trip check.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (Xenova/texify, linked, ONNX build of vikp/texify), task (image-to-text, vision-encoder-decoder), output (LaTeX), architecture (Donut-Swin + mBART), quantisation (q8, merged decoder for cached generation), backend (WASM), download (~320 MB) and license (cc-by-sa-4.0, base model). The How-it-works block shows the real pipeline call. No invented API.
rubric.notes: transformers.js is loaded via the shared lib/webai.js; dtype q8 resolves the q8 encoder + q8 merged decoder.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/texify', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:320}). First visit shows an explicit Download (~320 MB); the ONNX encoder+decoder are cached by transformers.js so a returning visit auto-initialises (checking -> ready) with NO Download click. Verified: fresh profile showed Download; clicking it enabled the controls.
rubric.notes: The never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return hold via the shared loader; inference is off the main thread in a module Worker.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (~320 MB) -> ready (controls enabled) -> reading (status) -> streaming LaTeX -> done (copy + re-typeset preview + readout). Recognition failures surface on the page in a role=status .err region (not only the console); clipboard-copy failure is caught and shown on the button. Verified download -> ready -> recognise end-to-end.
rubric.notes: The image preview shows what was read before the LaTeX appears; the MathJax preview is progressive (optional) and never blocks the copyable LaTeX.

rubric.evidence: Four real input paths: paste from clipboard (Ctrl/Cmd+V), drag-and-drop, a Choose-image picker, and four clickable sample-equation thumbnails; plus a Copy button for the LaTeX. Every control is labelled and keyboard-operable; the drop zone gives drag feedback; no dead controls.
rubric.notes: Drop rejects non-image files with a readable message; paste picks the first image item off the clipboard.

rubric.evidence: The LaTeX is the model's real streamed decode; the readout shows the live backend + latency + token count, and the tokens visibly form. The page is HONEST about limits: a best-effort single-equation recogniser that nails structure but can slip on an ambiguous character (E vs H) or dense/handwritten input - and the re-typeset preview makes any slip easy to spot and fix.
rubric.notes: The samples are first-party procedural PNGs whose LaTeX was verified, not asserted; nothing is hard-coded.

rubric.evidence: The LaTeX output is aria-live; the status is role=status; every button has an accessible name; the sample thumbnails carry alt text describing each equation; the file input is a real labelled <input type=file>. The recovered LaTeX is itself an accessibility win (a screen reader / MathML pipeline can speak it).
rubric.notes: The re-typeset SVG preview is aria-hidden (decorative duplicate of the copyable LaTeX, which is the accessible source of truth).

rubric.evidence: The drop zone, tools, sample thumbnails, preview and output all use fluid widths and wrap; the LaTeX body wraps (word-break) and the re-typeset preview scrolls horizontally in its own container so a wide equation never breaks the page. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate 10/10), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Sample thumbnails are capped in height; the preview image is max-width:100%.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; the LaTeX streams token by token so the page shows progress rather than freezing. The ~320 MB download is one-time (cached).
rubric.notes: Generation is autoregressive on a ~240 MB decoder, so a full equation takes a few seconds on WASM - the streaming output + 'Reading…' status keep it honest; WebGPU would speed it up (follow-up).

guidanceConsulted.query: paste image from clipboard, drag and drop file, accessible input paths
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offer multiple accessible input paths (a real file input + drag-drop + paste), give drag feedback, and label everything.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — clipboard paste (Ctrl/Cmd+V), a drop zone with drag feedback, a labelled <input type=file> + Choose-image button, and clickable alt-texted sample thumbnails.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: four input paths; drop rejects non-images; samples carry equation alt text

guidanceConsulted.query: stream generated tokens without blocking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Do heavy generative work off the main thread and stream partial output to keep the UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation runs in a module Worker and streams LaTeX tokens to the page via a TextStreamer; the main thread never blocks on the decode.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: LaTeX forms live; UI stays responsive during a multi-second decode

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live results and graceful clipboard copy
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic results in aria-live, keep the accessible source of truth textual, and handle clipboard failure gracefully.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the LaTeX output is aria-live and is the accessible source of truth; the SVG preview is aria-hidden; clipboard copy is wrapped so a denied write shows 'Copy failed' instead of throwing.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: results readable as text; copy degrades gracefully

openQuestions: Would a WebGPU path meaningfully cut the multi-second autoregressive decode for longer equations?
Could a lightweight pre-crop / binarisation step improve accuracy on photographed (vs rendered) equations with uneven lighting?
followUpGoals: Add a WebGPU backend option for faster generation on capable devices.
Offer a MathML export alongside LaTeX for direct accessibility-tree insertion.
Add a light auto-crop so a user can photograph a page and the demo isolates the equation before recognising.
summary: Math-formula OCR (texify, Donut-Swin + mBART) — a distinct vision-language family: a dedicated equation-image -> LaTeX recogniser, vs the built document/line/scene OCR (nougat/trocr/mgp-str). Proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM q8 (Pythagoras, a fraction, a definite integral, the quadratic formula all recovered with correct structure), streaming the LaTeX token by token with a re-typeset preview. ~320 MB, cc-by-sa-4.0. Paste/drop/choose/sample input; index demo complete + verified 10/10. Honest that it's a best-effort single-equation recogniser (structure solid, occasional character slip). A WebGPU path, MathML export, and auto-crop for photos are recorded follow-ups.
